approval.note: Diagnose the missing automatic push-triggered workflows without assuming the token cause. Preserve exact-SHA validation, avoid duplicate deployments, and require a reviewed recovery path before changing authentication or workflow triggers.

metadata.observed_limit: After PR #110 merged, GitHub created no automatic workflow runs for the merge SHA even though validate and production-readback declare push-to-main triggers. The exact Pages build and production readback had to be invoked explicitly. The cause was not established.
does_not_establish: that a specific GitHub token type caused the missing workflow runs
that every future merge will miss workflows
authorization to weaken branch protection or exact-SHA evidence
authorization to duplicate deployments or bypass review

title: Restore automatic exact-SHA post-merge evidence for Commonworld
goal: Determine why an accepted Commonworld merge can update main without creating the declared push-triggered validation and production-readback runs, then establish one idempotent, observable and fail-closed path that automatically produces exact-merge-SHA CI, Pages deployment and public readback evidence.

acceptance.assertion: A bounded test matrix distinguishes merge actor, authentication path, merge method and GitHub event type, and records which combinations do or do not create push-triggered runs without mutating production repeatedly.
The selected explanation is supported by GitHub-owned event, check-run or workflow evidence and is not inferred solely from token folklore or timing.
Every accepted main update produces one authoritative validation result and one exact-SHA deployment/readback chain, either through native push triggers or an explicitly designed dispatch bridge.
Missing-trigger recovery is retry-safe, refuses stale SHAs, does not duplicate a successful deployment, and leaves an auditable receipt.
The repair does not weaken review, branch protection, CI, deployment identity or public-readback requirements.
Closeout includes a reviewed diff, green tests, a real merged canary or equivalent bounded proof, exact-SHA Pages deployment and public production readback.
